clusters.rationale: Both refer to involuntary removal by extraterrestrial beings; alien-abduction has 1687 reports vs 58, making it the clear canonical form.
Rename-only candidate: overly clinical name for a well-known phenomenon.
All refer to the classic saucer/disc configuration; disc-ufo has highest reports (1285), but flying-saucer is the most iconic name; flat-disc and disk-with-dome are variants of the same archetype.
Both describe two spheres/discs connected by a bridge; barbell-ufo has 23 reports vs dumbbell-ufo's 23, same count but barbell is more common terminology.
Tall Grey Alien is a subtype of Grey Alien (5-7 ft variant); Zeta Reticulan is the same archetype with origin attribution; grey-alien is the canonical form with 640 reports.
Both describe coordinated groups of luminous objects in formation; light-formation-ufo has 893 reports vs multi-light-formation's 754.
Glowing orb is a subset of nocturnal lights (self-luminous spheres at night); nocturnal-light is broader and has 5845 reports.
All describe spherical objects; orb-ufo has 2613 reports (highest), sphere-ufo has 956, metallic-sphere is a material variant; orb-ufo is canonical.
All describe triangular/delta-shaped craft; triangular-ufo has 1291 reports (highest); black-triangle, silent-triangle, arrowhead, pyramid, and wing are all variants of the same morphology.
All describe elongated cylindrical craft; cigar-ufo has 499 reports (highest), but cylindrical-ufo is the more general term; oblong and tubular are variants.
All describe geometric box-like shapes; rectangle-ufo has 326 reports (highest); cube and square are specific variants of rectangular geometry.
All describe V-shaped or chevron configurations; chevron-ufo has 181 reports, boomerang has 68, v-shaped has 112; chevron is the most precise term.
Morphing and shape-shifting describe dynamic form changes; pulsating describes brightness changes; these are distinct phenomena—do not cluster.
Insectoid and Mantis are distinct alien types, not reptilians—do not cluster; keeping separate.

clusters.canonical_name: Close Encounter of the Fourth Kind
clusters.rationale: CE4 explicitly refers to abduction; alien-abduction is a separate phenomenon category, not a duplicate.
Light-being and plasma-ufo describe luminous non-corporeal entities; energy-being is the broader category with 252 reports; light-being has 115, plasma-ufo has 71.
African sites and Nazca Lines are specific examples/evidence for ancient astronaut theory, not separate phenomena—they are manifestations of the theory.
UFO Nuclear Connection is a specific subset of EM effects (at nuclear facilities); electromagnetic-effects-ufo is broader with 547 reports.
Rod-shaped-ufo and rod/skyfish refer to the same elongated fast-moving objects; rod-skyfish has 27 reports vs rod-shaped-ufo's 16.
Both refer to small green-skinned humanoids; green-alien has 24 reports vs little-green-men's 9; green-alien is more descriptive.
Galactic Federation is a specific entity/concept within contactee narratives, not a separate phenomenon; contactee-movement is the broader category.

rename_only.rationale: Overly clinical and jargon-heavy; 'crop circles' is the standard colloquial term.

rename_only.current_name: Close Encounter of the First Kind
rename_only.rationale: Hynek classification is technical; most people search 'UFO sighting' or 'CE1'.

rename_only.current_name: Close Encounter of the Second Kind
rename_only.rationale: Technical classification; 'physical traces' or 'CE2' is more searchable.

rename_only.current_name: Close Encounter of the Third Kind
rename_only.rationale: Technical classification; 'entity encounter' or 'CE3' is more intuitive.

rename_only.current_name: Close Encounter of the Fifth Kind
rename_only.rationale: Technical classification; 'human-initiated contact' or 'CE5' is clearer.
Overly clinical; 'consciousness-UFO connection' is more natural.
Abbreviation 'ET' is jargon; spell out for clarity.
Spell out 'ET' and use 'theory' for consistency.
More intuitive; 'landing traces' is how people search for this.
Technical term; 'memory suppression' is more descriptive.
Minor: 'subterrestrial' (from within Earth) is clearer than 'ultraterrestrial'.
